10:30 AM – Literature Synthesis: Systematic Review of Arthritis Diagnostic Criteria  
The researcher spends 75 minutes updating their systematic review of arthritis diagnostic criteria used in population surveys since 2010. They categorize studies by whether they used the American College of Rheumatology (ACR) criteria, self-reported physician diagnosis, or symptom-based algorithms. They note that 68% of published prevalence studies rely solely on self-report, which they argue leads to both overdiagnosis (e.g., confusing muscle strain with osteoarthritis) and underdiagnosis (e.g., dismissing pain in younger adults as “normal”). They begin coding studies for risk-of-bias assessment using the Cochrane tool.
